title: Fixed Header and Footer Layout
description: Create a grid with 3 rows: a 100px header, a flexible main content area (1fr), and a 80px footer. Use `min-height: 100vh` on the container so the layout fills the viewport.
hint: Use `grid-template-rows: 100px 1fr 80px`. The `1fr` middle row will fill all remaining vertical space after the fixed rows are accounted for.

title: Mixed Content Grid with Row Spanning
description: Create a 3-column × 3-row grid where item 1 spans all 3 rows on the left, items 2-3 span the top row on the right, and items 4-8 fill the remaining cells. Use `grid-template-rows` with appropriate row heights and `grid-row` for spanning.

starterCode.html:
Sidebar (span 3 rows)
Top Right
Top Right
Cell 4
Cell 5
Cell 6
Cell 7
Cell 8

hint: For the sidebar to span all 3 rows, use `grid-row: span 3` or `grid-row: 1 / 4`. The column definition is already provided — just focus on the row spanning.

title: Dynamic Blog with Auto-Rows and Spanning
description: Build a blog listing page that uses `grid-auto-rows` for variable-height posts. Create a 2-column layout where some posts span 2 columns (featured) and others vary in height. Use explicit rows for the first 2 rows (200px each) and implicit rows for subsequent posts (150px). Make the featured post span across 2 columns.

starterCode.html:
Featured Post
(spans 2 cols)
Tall Post
Regular Post
Regular Post
Another Featured
(spans 2 cols)
Regular Post
Tall Post
Regular Post

hint: 1. Use `grid-template-rows: repeat(2, 200px)` for the first 2 rows. 2. Use `grid-auto-rows: 150px` for implicit rows. 3. Featured posts use `grid-column: span 2`. 4. Without `grid-auto-flow: dense`, featured posts will create gaps — try adding it.
